notes.claim: Approximately 575,000 Japanese were interned in Siberia
notes.context: 根据厚生劳动省的调查，战争结束时在满洲、朝鲜、库页岛等地的日本军民约有57.5万人被苏联送往西伯利亚、中亚等地。 According to Russian sources, there are approximately 500,000 people. Japan estimates that approximately 55,000 detainees died.

sources.title: Information on Siberian internment (Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare) (日语)

notes.claim: 被拘留者被迫在恶劣条件下劳动
notes.context: Internees were engaged in hard labor in Siberian coal mines, railway construction (Baikal-Amur Railway, etc.), deforestation, and construction work.许多人因极度寒冷、食物不足、衣服不足、缺乏医疗而死亡。 In 2015, the ``Siberian Internment Related Materials'' held by the Maizuru Hikiage Memorial Museum were added to UNESCO's ``Memory of the World'' list.
notes.verdict: 历史事实（联合国教科文组织登记）

sources.title: 世界记忆——二战后日本士兵被拘留的文献

notes.claim: 苏联的拘禁违反了《日内瓦公约》
notes.context: The 1929 Geneva Convention (Convention on the Treatment of Prisoners of War) requires the prompt repatriation of prisoners of war after hostilities have ended. Although the Soviet Union had not ratified the treaty, Japan had ratified it, and the application of general principles of international humanitarian law was at issue. 《波茨坦公告》第九条还规定“迅速遣返”日本军事人员。

sources.title: 关于战俘待遇的日内瓦公约（1929）
